title: pterocarpus cambodianus
intro: Pterocarpus cambodianus, also known as the Cambodian teak or Cambodian padauk, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a valuable medicinal plant with various properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or extracts, which can be applied topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
